gates.title: Protocol close rejects malformed outcomes instead of recording false failures
evidence_required: nexo_task_close returns an explicit error for invalid close outcomes
Malformed close requests leave the protocol task status and closed_at fields untouched
Regression coverage pins the non-mutation contract

gates.title: Protocol and Cortex tools reject malformed task types instead of silently changing their meaning
evidence_required: nexo_task_open and nexo_confidence_check reject invalid task_type values
nexo_cortex_check and nexo_cortex_decide reject invalid task_type values
Regression coverage pins both the JSON and text-path error surfaces

gates.title: Cortex decisions reject malformed impact levels instead of silently promoting them to high
evidence_required: nexo_cortex_decide returns an explicit error for invalid impact_level values
Persisted cortex evaluations only receive canonical impact levels
Regression coverage pins the invalid-impact rejection path

gates.title: Internal DB helpers enforce the same task-type, close-outcome, and impact-level contracts as the public tools
evidence_required: create_protocol_task validates task_type before writing a row
close_protocol_task validates close outcomes before writing a row
create_cortex_evaluation validates impact_level before writing a row

gates.title: Package, docs, plugin artifacts, and website tell the same v5.3.11 story
evidence_required: Root package and integration artifacts all point to 5.3.11
README, changelog, llms, and release contract mention protocol/cortex contract hardening clearly
Website home, blog, changelog, llms, and sitemap are updated to 5.3.11
